title: Lentivirus production
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"15mtl","text":"This protocol describes the production of lentivirus in HEK293T cells for the transduction of primary neuronal cultures.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"43irj","text":"Cells","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b7pod","text":"HEK293T cells (early passage) (CRL-3216, ATCC)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"87avl","text":"Plasmids","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1rh5u","text":"Gene of interest in lentiviral backbone ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"486nj","text":"Accessory plasmids: pMDLG/pRRE, pIVS-VSVG, pRSV-Rev","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"63qai","text":"Reagents","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7lnak","text":"FuGENE transfection reagent (E2311, Promega)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dhlh7","text":"Neurobasal medium (21103-049, Gibco)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"37lff","text":"Poly-L-lysine for coating 6-well plates (P2636, Sigma-Aldrich)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5bdhb","text":"Consumables","type":"header-three","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9kmfn","text":"6-well tissue culture plates","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fnmo6","text":"0.45 μm filter units","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Coat 6-well plates with poly-L-lysine for 1 h, and wash twice with PBS.
Seed early-passage (< P10) HEK293T cells onto coated 6-well plates to reach ~70–80% confluency the next day.

Prepare a DNA mixture per well: a. In 200 μL Opti-MEM, combine: - 1.5 μg plasmid encoding the gene of interest (pFUGW, pJHUG, or pJHUIC) - 0.5 μg each of accessory plasmids (pMDLG/pRRE, pIVS-VSVG, pRSV-Rev) b. Add 12 μL FuGENE HD transfection reagent to the DNA solution. c. Mix gently by vortexing. d. Incubate at room temperature for 10–15 min to allow complex formation.
Remove HEK cells' medium and replace it with 1.8 mL of fresh medium per well.
Add the transfection mixture to each well and incubate overnight at 37°C, 5% CO₂.
The next morning, remove the transfection medium and replace it with 2 mL neurobasal medium or HEK cells' medium per well.
Incubate cells for 36–48 h to allow virus production.

Collect the culture medium containing lentivirus. Filter the collected medium through a 0.45 μm filter to remove cellular debris. Use the viral supernatant immediately for neuronal transduction, or aliquot and store at -80°C.
Add 20–40 μL viral supernatant per cloning ring of primary neuronal culture to achieve efficient transduction.
creator: Akio Mori
